creator: markj@FreeBSD.org
text:
(In reply to crest from comment #40)
> In the worst case the "nobody" user in one jail (or the host) passes it's root directory to the "root" user in a malicous jail.

If this channel exists, then what stops the jailed root user from creating a setuid shell, opening it, passing the fd to the nobody user, who fexecve()s it?

This general scenario is described in the NOTES section of jail(8).
